profiles.bio: Passionate software engineer focused on system architectures and algorithms.

companies.name: Google
companies.overview: Google LLC is an American multinational technology company focusing on artificial intelligence, search engine technology, online advertising, cloud computing, computer software, and quantum computing.
companies.hiringProcess: Consists of a rigorous DSA-focused online assessment, followed by 3-4 rounds of algorithmic and architectural design problem-solving with a final behavioral fitment round.
companies.eligibility: B.Tech/M.Tech/MCA with 70%+ or 7.0 CGPA, no active backlogs.
companies.ctcBreakdown: Base: 22L, RSUs: 15L (vested over 4 years), Sign-on: 3L, Performance Bonus: 15%
companies.recentTrends: Focusing heavily on search quality systems, real-time data ingestion pipelines, and ML/AI model integrations.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.overview: Microsoft Corporation is an American multinational technology corporation producing computer software, consumer electronics, personal computers, and cloud services.
companies.hiringProcess: Begins with an online technical test, followed by virtual on-sites emphasizing operating systems concepts, data structures, and structural system design.
companies.eligibility: Open to all engineering streams with CGPA > 7.5.
companies.ctcBreakdown: Base: 18L, Stock: 10L (over 4 years), Joining Bonus: 2.5L, Performance: 10-15%
companies.recentTrends: Deepening integrations with OpenAI services, cloud containerizations, and multi-tenant SaaS services.

companies.name: Amazon
companies.overview: Amazon.com, Inc. is an American multinational technology company focusing on e-commerce, cloud computing, online advertising, digital streaming, and artificial intelligence.
companies.hiringProcess: Very structured hiring process focused heavily on the 16 Leadership Principles combined with classic DSA and architectural problems.
companies.eligibility: B.Tech/B.E/M.S in Computer Science or related fields.
companies.ctcBreakdown: Base: 16.5L, Stocks: 10L, First year sign-on: 3.5L, Second year sign-on: 4L
companies.recentTrends: Focusing on distributed caching, high throughput logistics queues, and robust microservices.

companies.name: Atlassian
companies.overview: Atlassian Corporation is an Australian software company that develops products for software development, project management, and collaborative team processes.
companies.hiringProcess: Starts with standard OA, followed by highly interactive live coding where code cleanliness, test cases, and OOP structure are scrutinized alongside algorithmic optimality.
companies.recentTrends: Collaboration platform scalability, real-time text sync, and AI-enabled JIRA integrations.

companies.name: Uber
companies.overview: Uber Technologies, Inc. is an American technology provider of ride-hailing, food delivery, and freight transportation systems.
companies.hiringProcess: Among the hardest technical interviews. Incorporates standard algorithmic rounds along with a dedicated machine coding or multi-threaded logic round.
companies.eligibility: Exceptional problem-solving skills, open branch, but solid CS foundations.
companies.recentTrends: High scale geofencing, dynamic surge pricing systems, and real-time navigation algorithms.

companies.name: Netflix
companies.overview: Netflix, Inc. is an American subscription video-on-demand over-the-top streaming service and production company.
companies.hiringProcess: Very focused on independent execution capability, deep architectural insights, and high compliance with the Netflix Freedom & Responsibility culture deck.
companies.eligibility: Extensive software experience or highly outstanding project history.
companies.recentTrends: Edge computing nodes, adaptive video encoding, and advanced AI recommendation layers.

codingQuestions.title: Two Sum
codingQuestions.content:
Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

codingQuestions.title: Two Sum
codingQuestions.content:
Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

codingQuestions.title: LRU Cache
codingQuestions.content:
Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache.

Implement the LRUCache class:
- LRUCache(int capacity) Initialize the LRU cache with positive size capacity.
- int get(int key) Return the value of the key if the key exists, otherwise return -1.
- void put(int key, int value) Update the value of the key if the key exists. Otherwise, add the key-value pair to the cache. If the number of keys exceeds the capacity from this operation, evict the least recently used key.

codingQuestions.title: Merge k Sorted Lists
codingQuestions.content:
You are given an array of k linked-lists lists, each linked-list is sorted in ascending order.

Merge all the linked-lists into one sorted linked-list and return it.

codingQuestions.title: Valid Parentheses
codingQuestions.content: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.
codingQuestions.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 10^4
s consists of parentheses only

codingQuestions.title: Longest Palindromic Substring
codingQuestions.content: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring in s.
codingQuestions.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 1000
s consists of English letters and digits

submissions.feedback: Your code ran correctly and satisfied all default unit-tests. (Estimated complexity: O(N) Time, O(1) Space)

notes.title: System Design - Rate Limiter
notes.content:
Topics discussed: Token Bucket vs Leaky Bucket vs Sliding Window Log algorithms.
Token bucket is best for bursty traffic because it permits instant execution of accumulated tokens. Leaky bucket smooths out requests at a constant flow rate. Redis sorted sets are perfect for implementing sliding window rate limiters.

notes.title: Dynamic Programming Patterns
notes.content:
Main patterns:
1. 0/1 Knapsack
2. Unbounded Knapsack
3. Fibonacci sequence
4. Longest Common Subsequence (LCS)
5. Shortest Path grid traversal

Optimizing space complexity from O(N*M) to O(Min(N,M)) is highly appreciated during interviews.
